ifc 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Changelog

Create an interval between two IFC dates

Source: R/interval.R
ifc_interval.Rd

ifc_interval() records the span between a start and end ifc_date. Dividing by a unit string returns the number of complete or fractional units in the span.

Usage

ifc_interval(start, end)

Arguments

start, end

ifc_date vectors (or anything coercible to ifc_date). Recycled to a common length.

Value

An ifc_interval object.

Details

IFC's fixed-length units (7 days/week, 28 days/month) mean that spans between regular IFC dates divide exactly with no fractional remainder — an advantage over Gregorian intervals where month lengths vary.

Examples

iv <- ifc_interval(ifc_ymd(2024, 1, 1), ifc_ymd(2024, 7, 1))
iv / "day"    # integer days between the two dates
#> [1] 169
iv / "week"   # days / 7
#> [1] 24.14286
iv / "month"  # days / 28
#> [1] 6.035714
